zh.Arkel, Giessenlanden, South Holland, Net: 荷蘭-拉藍德希森蘭登阿可爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jan IV van Arkel於大約1305年出生在荷蘭-拉藍德格利姆時，他的父親Jan Herbanen Van Arkel III31歲，母親Mabilia de Wael van Voorne van Arkel33歲。他於1327年與Irmgard von Kleve結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1360年5月14日在荷蘭霍蘭格利姆享年56歲，埋葬在荷蘭霍蘭格利姆。

zh-hans.Arkel, Giessenlanden, South Holland, Net: 荷兰-拉藍德希森蘭登阿可爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jan IV van Arkel于大约1305年出生在荷兰-拉藍德格利姆时，他的父亲Jan Herbanen Van Arkel III31岁，母亲Mabilia de Wael van Voorne van Arkel33岁。他于1327年与Irmgard von Kleve结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1360年5月14日在荷兰霍蘭格利姆享年56岁，埋葬在荷兰霍蘭格利姆。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Arkel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Yoram, Yosef, Amram, Ari, Avi, Gitty, Haim, Ilan, Moshe, Yaacov, Yaakov.
Jewish: from the Biblical placename Ariel, meaning ‘lion of God’ in Hebrew (compare   Arie  ). It is mentioned in the prophecies of Ezra (8:16) and Isaiah (29:1–2).

variants.name: Arie
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the personal name Arye, meaning ‘lion’ in Hebrew.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Jan IV van Arkel was born about 1305, in Gorinchem, South Holland, Netherlands, his father, Jan Herbanen Van Arkel III, was 31 and his mother, Mabilia de Wael van Voorne van Arkel, was 33. He married Irmgard von Kleve in 1327. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 14 May 1360, in Gorinchem, Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 56, and was buried in Gorinchem, Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jan IV van Arkel est né environ 1305, à Gorinchem, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Jan Herbanen Van Arkel III, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Mabilia de Wael van Voorne van Arkel, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Irmgard von Kleve en 1327. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 14 mai 1360, à Gorinchem, Holland, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Gorinchem, Holland, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jan IV van Arkel um 1305 in Gorinchem, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jan Herbanen Van Arkel III 31 und seine Mutter Mabilia de Wael van Voorne van Arkel 33. Er hat 1327 Irmgard von Kleve geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 14. Mai 1360 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Gorinchem, Holland, Niederlande und wurde in Gorinchem, Holland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jan IV van Arkel è nato il circa 1305 nel Gorinchem, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Jan Herbanen Van Arkel III, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Mabilia de Wael van Voorne van Arkel, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Irmgard von Kleve nel 1327. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 14 maggio 1360, nel Gorinchem, Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolto a Gorinchem, Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.Gorinchem, South Holland, Netherlands: オランダズイド-hオランドゴリンケム
ja.lifeSummary: Jan IV van Arkelが約1305年にオランダズイド-hオランドゴリンケムで生まれた時，父Jan Herbanen Van Arkel IIIは31歳，母Mabilia de Wael van Voorne van Arkelは33歳であった。1327年にIrmgard von Kleveと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1360年5月14日にオランダホーランドゴリンケムで56歳で死去し，オランダホーランドゴリンケムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jan IV van Arkel(이)가 대략 1305년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 고린체에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jan Herbanen Van Arkel III(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Mabilia de Wael van Voorne van Arkel(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1327년 Irmgard von Kleve(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1360년 5월 14일 네덜란드 홀랜드 고린체에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 홀랜드 고린체에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jan IV van Arkel nasceu aproximadamente 1305, em Gorinchem, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Jan Herbanen Van Arkel III, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Mabilia de Wael van Voorne van Arkel, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Irmgard von Kleve em 1327. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de maio de 1360, em Gorinchem, Holland, Países Baixos, com 56 anos, e foi sepultado em Gorinchem, Holland, Países Baixos.

ro.Arkel, Giessenlanden, South Holland, Net: Arkel, Giessenlanden, Zuid-Holland, Ţările de Jos

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jan IV van Arkel родился около 1305 г. в местности горинчем, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Jan Herbanen Van Arkel III было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Mabilia de Wael van Voorne van Arkel, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Irmgard von Kleve в 1327 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 14 мая 1360 г. в местности горинчем, холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности горинчем, холланд, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jan IV van Arkel nació aproximadamente 1305, en Gorinchem, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Jan Herbanen Van Arkel III, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Mabilia de Wael van Voorne van Arkel, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Irmgard von Kleve en 1327. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 14 de mayo de 1360, en Gorinchem, Holland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultado en Gorinchem, Holland, Países Bajos.
